mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress corporate earnings expectations and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that sustains corporate revenue trajectories, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that boosts near-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of months where inflation exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive power vanishes when inflation is already above 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate profit expansion, establishing a persistent positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The GDP growth signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over consecutive 4-year rolling windows, or forward returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production indicate a supply-side contraction that forces immediate corporate earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces positive forward returns in >60% of triggered instances or fails to maintain a hit rate above 55% in out-of-sample testing.
